mission.title: fluent-bit: Docker_mode to recombine multiline records in json-log from docker
mission.description: Docker_mode to recombine multiline records in json-log from docker. Requested by 8+ users.

steps.title: Check current fluent-bit deployment
steps.description:
Verify your fluent-bit version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
helm list -n fluent-bit 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working fluent-bit installation.

steps.title: Review fluent-bit configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant fluent-bit configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl get configmap -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=fluent-bit
```
**Problem**
If the application in kubernetes logs multiline messages, docker split this message to multiple json-log messages.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Docker_mode to recombine multiline records in json-log from…
steps.description:
Use Parser to verify if line is valid first line while using dockermode.

For example input:
```
{"log":"2020-03-24 Test line of first log\n"}
{"log":"           Another line of first log\n"}
{"log":"2020-03-24 Test line of second log\n"}

```

And regex: `Regex
```yaml
`docker_mode: 0n` shall - recombine split Docker log lines before passing them to any parser as configured above.  

I would expect it will apply to this case as well, however I it does not. Below I provided my configuration.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

in_tail/docker_mode - shall have the possibility to read docker's json-log as a stream of original text. json parser, here is just pre-processor that will buffer the "log" key,  so multiline regexp patterns can be used later.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

I believe this problem can be avoided if:

1. docker logs are s
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl get events -n fluent-bit --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Docker_mode to recombine multiline records in json-log from…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Use Parser to verify if line is valid first line while using dockermode.
codeSnippets:
`docker_mode: 0n` shall - recombine split Docker log lines before passing them to any parser as configured above.  

I would expect it will apply to this case as well, however I it does not. Below I provided my configuration.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

in_tail/docker_mode - shall have the possibility to read docker's json-log as a stream of original text. json parser, here is just pre-processor that will buffer the "log" key,  so multiline regexp patterns can be used later.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

I believe this problem can be avoided if:

1. docker logs are sent directly to fluentd (docker fluentd driver, https://docs.docker.com/config/containers/logging/fluentd/)
2. docker logs are sent to journal/syslog etc

However:

- on hosted kubernetes platforms, y

Use Parser to verify if line is valid first line while using dockermode.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with fluent-bit installed or the issue environment reproducible.
